title: Her Win on the Table Beside Yours, Both Real
subtitle: A visualisation for seeing another woman's success alongside your own without one diminishing the other
excerpt: A short visualisation for the moment when comparison is running. Two things on the same table: her win and yours. Neither larger, neither in competition with the other. Both real.

practice.whenToUse: Use when another woman's financial success has landed as a comparison and you want to reset before the story builds.

content.text: A short visualisation for placing two things beside each other without one cancelling the other out. Her win. Yours. The practice is about proximity without competition.
Sit quietly. Close your eyes or soften your gaze.
Picture a large, clean table — plain surface, no clutter. Place her win on the table as an object. Don't make it small, don't make it large. Just an object. Real.
Now place yours on the table beside it. The thing you want most in your own money situation — the version of success that belongs to you. Real, too.
Look at both objects. They don't touch. They don't compete for space. The table is big enough for both.
Stay with this for two minutes. Neither object has to move. Neither shrinks because the other is there.
